More Notes: Population living in areas with high climate suitability for dengue virus transmission.
sourcematerial: We found an expansion in climate suitability in North America, East Asia and the Mediterranean basin, where with few exceptions, endemicity is not yet established. Globally, we estimated that the population in areas with high climate suitability has grown by approximately 2.5 billion.

More Notes: High climate suitability areas are associated with an 11-fold greater dengue incidence compared to low climate suitability areas.
The global population living in high-risk areas increased from 48.8% to 60.0%, translating to 2.5 billion more people in high climate suitability zones.
Land area with high climate suitability increased from 38.4% to 39.5% globally.
Change in proportion of high climate suitability for DENV transmission
sourcematerial: Globally, the proportion of high and very high density areas with high climate suitability for DENV transmission increased from 58.5% to 66.2% (Δ = 7.7 pp, 90% CI = 4.7–13.0) and from 48.1% to 63.6% (Δ = 15.5 pp, 90% CI = 14.1-–17.0), respectively.; larger relative increases in climate suitability were observed for upper-middle and high income economies. Over the last four decades, the proportion of land area with high climate suitability increased from 31.6% to 32.9% (Δ = 1.3 pp, 90% CI = 0.2–2.4) and from 20.5% to 22.2% (Δ = 1.6 pp, 90% CI = 0.3–2.5) in upper-middle income and high income economies, respectively.; Interestingly, we found that temperate climate zones had the largest net change, with an increase in the proportion of land area with high climate suitability from 25.2% to 30.5% (Δ = 5.3 pp, 90% CI = 3.2–6.2). The tropical climate zones, which were estimated to have the greatest share of high climate suitability, had a smaller increase from 87.5% to 90.1% (Δ = 2.6 pp, 90% CI = 1.3–3.5).
